other_findings: B cells emerge in prenatal skin from around 10 PCW, accompanying thymus, bone marrow and spleen formation
B cells are part of the broad 'B cell' category in the fetal scope of the atlas
Multiple B cell developmental stages are identified: Pre pro B cell, Pro B cell, Pre B cell, B cell
B cells and their precursors detected in skin as young as 8 PCW by scRNAseq (Popescu et al., 2019)
CD24+CD19+ B cell precursors consistently found in fetal skin specimens (Dhariwala et al., 2020)
Plasma cells, but not B cells, are present in steady-state adult skin
evidence_quotes: B cells and T cells emerged later, accompanying thymus, bone marrow and spleen formation from around 10 PCW

B-cells have not been observed in fetal skin by flow cytometry or immunofluorescence using CD19 and CD20 as markers. However, using scRNAseq, B-cells and their precursors have been detected in skin as young as 8 PCW
Detection of cells in various stages of B-cell differentiation in first-trimester skin indicates that B-cell precursors are released from the liver into circulation where they can then seed tissue
